zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirkton於1294年出生在英國薩默塞特梅利特時，她的父親Hugh Fleury20歲，母親Aleonore Beauthamp20歲。她於1325年與George de Meriet在英國薩默塞特梅利特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1370年6月29日在英國享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirkton于1294年出生在英国薩默塞特梅利特时，她的父亲Hugh Fleury20岁，母亲Aleonore Beauthamp20岁。她于1325年与George de Meriet在英国薩默塞特梅利特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1370年6月29日在英国享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Flory
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: variant of   Fleury   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
French: from a medieval vernacular form of the Latin personal name Florius, a derivative of Florus, which is from florus ‘blooming, flowering’.
South German (in Austria mainly Flöry): from a short form of the personal name Florian . In part, this is a Germanized form of French Fleury (compare 1 above).

variants.name: Fleury
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Lucien, Yvon, Andre, Gilles, Herve, Jacques, Michel, Adrien, Benoit, Camille, Evariste.

French and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Fleury, the name of several places in various parts of France which get their names from the Gallo-Roman personal name Florus (from Latin florus ‘blooming, flowering’) + the locative suffix -acum. The surname of French origin is also found in Germany. Compare Flory .

History: André Fleury from La Ronde-Haye in Manche, France, married Marie-Josèphe Tanguay in Saint-Vallier, QC, in 1760. — The surname was also brought to the US from Germany, having been taken there by Huguenot refugees (see   Flory  ).

nameMeanings.name: Fleury
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Lucien, Yvon, Andre, Gilles, Herve, Jacques, Michel, Adrien, Benoit, Camille, Evariste.

French and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Fleury, the name of several places in various parts of France which get their names from the Gallo-Roman personal name Florus (from Latin florus ‘blooming, flowering’) + the locative suffix -acum. The surname of French origin is also found in Germany. Compare Flory .

History: André Fleury from La Ronde-Haye in Manche, France, married Marie-Josèphe Tanguay in Saint-Vallier, QC, in 1760. — The surname was also brought to the US from Germany, having been taken there by Huguenot refugees (see   Flory  ).

variants.name: Florey
meanings: English: variant of   Fleury  .
Americanized form of German   Flory  .
German: altered form of French   Fleury  .

variants.name: Flora
meanings: Spanish, Portuguese, and Italian; Hungarian (Flóra): from the female personal name Flora, Flóra, from Latin flos, genitive floris ‘flower’.
Americanized form of French   Fleury   or German   Flory  .
Czech and Polish: from a vernacular short form of the Latin personal name Florianus (see Florian ).

variants.name: Flurry
meanings: English: variant of   Fleury  .
Perhaps also an Americanized form of French Fleury or of Austrian German Flöry (see Flory ).

variants.name: Flury
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Claus, Darrold, Fritz, Markus, Mathias.
English: variant of   Fleury  .
Swiss German: from a vernacular short form of the personal name Florian . In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the variant Fluri.

variants.name: Flory
meanings: English and French: variant of   Fleury   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
French: from a medieval vernacular form of the Latin personal name Florius, a derivative of Florus, which is from florus ‘blooming, flowering’.
South German (in Austria mainly Flöry): from a short form of the personal name Florian . In part, this is a Germanized form of French Fleury (compare 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirkton was born in 1294, in Merriott, Somerset, England, her father, Hugh Fleury, was 20 and her mother, Aleonore Beauthamp, was 20. She married George de Meriet in 1325, in Merriott, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 29 June 1370, in England, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirkton est née en 1294, à Merriott, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Fleury, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Aleonore Beauthamp, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé George de Meriet en 1325, à Merriott, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 29 juin 1370, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirkton 1294 in Merriott, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Fleury 20 und ihre Mutter Aleonore Beauthamp 20. Sie hat 1325 in Merriott, Somerset, England George de Meriet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 29. Juni 1370 im Alter von 76 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirkton è nata il nel 1294 nel Merriott, Somerset, England, sua padre, Hugh Fleury, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Aleonore Beauthamp, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato George de Meriet nel 1325, nel Merriott, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 29 giugno 1370, nel England, all'età di 76 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirktonが1294年にイギリスソメルセトメリオットで生まれた時，父Hugh Fleuryは20歳，母Aleonore Beauthampは20歳であった。1325年にイギリスソメルセトメリオットでGeorge de Merietと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1370年6月29日にイギリスで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirkton(이)가 1294년 영국 서머세트 머리엇에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Fleury(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Aleonore Beauthamp(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1325년 영국 서머세트 머리엇에서 George de Meriet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1370년 6월 29일 영국에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirkton nasceu em 1294, em Merriott, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Fleury, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Aleonore Beauthamp, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com George de Meriet em 1325, em Merriott, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 29 de junho de 1370, em Inglaterra, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Flory Fleury Kirketon Kirkton родилась в 1294 г. в местности мирриотт, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh Fleury было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Aleonore Beauthamp, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George de Meriet в 1325 г. в местности мирриотт, сомирсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 29 июня 1370 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 76 лет.
